newsSince: On June 22, 2026 China's Ministry of Commerce added MP Materials (and USA Rare Earth) to its export control list, barring Chinese exporters from supplying dual-use items to MP. As a US-domestic, Pentagon-backed (~15% owned) producer this is largely symbolic for sales but reinforces the strategic-independence narrative; MP rose ~10% since June 9 amid a sector rally on escalating China tensions. Prior DoD/Apple deals are 2025 history.

sources.label: StockTitan: China adds MP Materials to export control list (June 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens treats magnets as a physical-AI bottleneck and MP as the liquid strategic-materials proxy beside Mkango. China's June 22 export-control listing + sector rally sharpen the domestic-bottleneck value (restricts MP's inputs, not its US sales) and validate the choke-point thesis vs June 9 hold.
verdicts.bull: Only fully integrated US rare-earth magnet producer, Pentagon-backed with above-market price floor; China escalation underscores its strategic-bottleneck value.
verdicts.neutral: Listing is symbolic for a domestic producer; the move is partly sector beta, not company-specific gain.
verdicts.bear: Less hidden than micro-cap peers; trade-war volatility and input restrictions add policy risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: US rare-earth-independence policy and 10X magnet capacity ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: China retaliation on inputs; trade-policy volatility and execution on capacity
